observations.display: Amyloid PET: positive cortical binding (SUVR 2.1, cortical reference)

observations: MRI: moderate hippocampal atrophy, enlarged ventricles, posterior cingulate hypometabolism on FDG-PET
record.note: 72-year-old woman with 2-year history of progressive cognitive decline, initially presenting with short-term memory lapses and word-finding difficulties. Neuropsychological testing shows predominant amnestic pattern with relative sparing of executive function. Amyloid PET and CSF biomarkers (low Aβ42, elevated p-tau181 and t-tau) are consistent with Alzheimer's pathology. APOE ε4/ε4 genotype confers highest genetic risk. Comorbid T2DM (HbA1c 7.2%) may accelerate neurodegeneration. Depression precedes cognitive decline by ~1 year; currently managed with sertraline. Donepezil and memantine initiated 6 months ago with modest stabilization. Functional assessment: IADLs impaired (cooking, finances, medication management), ADLs intact. Caregiver (daughter) reports increasing sundowning episodes. Recommend follow-up in 3 months, consider lecanemab eligibility evaluation given APOE ε4/ε4 status and early disease stage.
